efficacyPairs.questionText: Sophie's school raised £4,567 for charity. What is the value of the digit 5 in this number?
efficacyPairs.subConceptName: Understanding the Value of Each Digit

efficacyPairs.questionText: A village fete raised exactly £3,845. The organiser rounds this to the nearest hundred for the newsletter. What amount does she write?
A park ranger counted seven thousand, two hundred and thirty-six trees in a forest. How is this number written in digits?
efficacyPairs.subConceptName: Reading & Writing Numbers in Words and Digits
efficacyPairs.lessonTeachSummary:
Big numbers are easier to read when you **split them into groups of three** from the right.

The left group is the **thousands**. The right group is the **ones**.

**163,500** = **one hundred and sixt
lessonLearningGoal: How to read and write numbers up to a million in words
Why we group digits in threes when writing large numbers

efficacyPairs.questionText: A road sign says the next town is twenty-three thousand, four hundred and fifty-six metres away. Write this distance in digits.
efficacyPairs.subConceptName: Reading & Writing Numbers in Words and Digits
efficacyPairs.lessonTeachSummary:
Big numbers are easier to read when you **split them into groups of three** from the right.

The left group is the **thousands**. The right group is the **ones**.

**163,500** = **one hundred and sixt
lessonLearningGoal: How to read and write numbers up to a million in words
Why we group digits in threes when writing large numbers
